node.Title: New year ushers in new wave of Snyk Technology Alliance Partner Program members
node.Description: With the new year, we’re excited to announce the second wave of new companies joining our Snyk Technology Alliance Partner Program (TAPP), which enables application and developer-focused software companies to build, integrate, and go-to-market as quickly as possible with Snyk solutions.

node.Title: Three ways Snyk made software supply chains more secure in 2022
node.Description: In 2022, Snyk continued to expand on our developer-centric approach to software security, by adding several key enhancements to the existing solutions — let's focus on three key capabilities introduced in the past year that can improve your software supply chain security.

node.Title: OSPO security evolution: The Kübler-Ross Model of open source
node.Description: Open Source Program Offices (OPSO) are popping up all over, in recognition of the facts on the ground: open source software (and I would argue open standards as well) plays an enormous role in building and maintaining the software that increasingly drives the planet.

node.Title: Snyk transformed cloud security in 2022 with developer-first solution
node.Description: Snyk Cloud security takes a developer-first, application-centric approach to finding and fixing vulnerabilities in cloud infrastructure from the time infrastructure is defined in code, through to when resources are running in the cloud.

node.Title: Snyk’s AppSec journey in 2022
node.Description: Learn about key AppSec investments Snyk made in 2022 to improve performance, add new ecosystems, and support the enterprise.

node.Title: Bolstering Snyk’s developer security platform in 2022
node.Description: Now that it's a new year, let’s take a look back at some of the amazing 2022 Snyk developer security platform highlights.

node.Title: Supply chain security incident at CircleCI: Rotate your secrets
node.Description: On January 4, CircleCI, an automated CI/CD pipeline setup tool, reported a security incident in their product by sharing an advisory. Learn about this incident, next steps, and about supply chain security.

node.Title: Creating AWS security efficiencies in IT
node.Description: In this post, we'll discuss several steps that your teams can take to create AWS efficiencies and streamline cloud security.

node.Title: You should be using HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) headers in your Node.js server
node.Description: Take an in-depth look at HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) headers to discover how they affect web security and why we should use them on Node.js. Then, learn how to enable HSTS inside a Node.js server.

node.Title: How to build a secure API gateway in Node.js
node.Description: In this article, we’ll build a secure API gateway from scratch using only Node.js and a couple of open source packages. All you need is basic knowledge of your terminal, Node.js version 14 or later, and JavaScript.

node.Title: 5 “no experience needed” tips for building secure applications
node.Description: Learn about the developer security tooling from Snyk that will help you level up your skills to find and fix security vulnerabilities in the code you write and the dependencies you use so you can build secure applications.

node.Title: Cloud security updates you need to know from re:Invent 2022
node.Description: Read all about the important cloud security updates from re:Invent 2022.

node.Title: Kubernetes network policy best practices
node.Description: This article will explore Kubernetes NetworkPolicy by creating an example network policy and examining its core parameters. Then, we’ll look at some common NetworkPolicy use cases and learn how to monitor them using kubectl. Finally, we’ll discover how to implement Container Network Interface (CNI) using third-party Kubernetes extensions.

node.Title: Panel recap: Breaking Bad Security Habits with Corey Quinn
node.Description: On December 8th, Clinton Herget and Simon Maple, Field CTOs at Snyk, had the opportunity to chat with Corey Quinn, Chief Cloud Economist at The Duckbill Group, podcast host, curator of "Last Week in AWS”, and snarky Twitter personality. Their conversation took a lot of fun turns, from ranting about the hour-long line to get coffee at AWS re:Invent, to Corey proclaiming that “SBOMs are a fantasy” (there’s more context to that… keep reading). We’ll be covering a few of the biggest highlights in this blog, but if you want to hear every Corey Quinn-ism from this conversation, watch the whole panel here.

node.Title: Building an application security battle plan: Home Alone edition
node.Description: In the spirit of the holiday season, we're laying out an application security “battle plan” for keeping out attackers — à la Kevin McAllister.

node.Title: Cloud Security Podcast awarded SANS Podcast of the Year
node.Description: Congratulations to Cloud Security Podcast for being given DMA's "Podcast of the Year" award!

node.Title: Exploring the Spring Security authorization bypass (CVE-2022-31692)
node.Description: Learn about the recently discovered Spring Security authorization bypass (CVE-2022-31692) and learn steps to mitigate.

node.Title: Snyk in 30: Open source security for Atlassian Bitbucket Cloud
node.Description: In our latest Snyk in 30, we showcased Snyk Open Source security with a focus on our integration with Atlassian Bitbucket Cloud.

node.Title: Top takeaways from re:Invent 2022
node.Description: Here are the major announcements at re:Invent that got our attention and some of the things Snyk was up to in Vegas.

node.Title: Unsafe deserialization vulnerability in SnakeYaml (CVE-2022-1471)
node.Description: SnakeYaml, a YAML 1.1 parser and emitter for Java, has been reported as vulnerable to CVE-2022-1471, a deserialization vulnerability that can lead to arbitrary code execution.

node.Title: Azure Bicep security fundamentals
node.Description: In those post, we look at Azure Bicep security fundamentals around inputs, outputs, secrets, Key Vault, and more.

node.Title: AWS re:Invent 2022: How Neiman Marcus transitioned to developer-first security
node.Description: During a discussion at AWS re:Invent 2022, Neiman Marcus Cyber Security Architect Omar Peerzada described how his team used Snyk to adopt a developer-first security strategy.

node.Title: Using Snyk reporting for data-driven security
node.Description: An overview of the benefits offered by Snyk's newly enhanced reporting capabilities.

node.Title: How to handle Node.js file uploads with Fastify
node.Description: In this tutorial, you'll use the Fastify framework in a Node.js application to set up file uploading. You'll learn how file uploads work on a typical server and how to handle file uploads on a Fastify server application.

node.Title: 5 best practices for React with TypeScript security
node.Description: Using TypeScript with React provides several advantages in application-building, including the option of simpler React components and better JavaScript XML (JSX) support for static type validation. Learn to stay safe with TypeScript security best practices.

node.Title: Why tool consolidation matters for developer security
node.Description: In this post we'll discuss some of the adverse effects organizations experience from tool sprawl, and the shift towards security tool consolidation.

node.Title: How to verify and secure your Mastodon account
node.Description: In this tutorial, we’ll walk through the process of verifying a Mastodon account, in order to gain a verification check mark and provide some legitimacy to the account.

node.Title: 3 tips for effective developer security training
node.Description: In this article, we’ll dig into three tips that can make your developer security education program better and ensure that you’re reaping all the advantages of the golden era of application security.

node.Title: Code injection vulnerabilities (CVSSv3 5.8) found in Snyk CLI and IDE plugins
node.Description: Learn how to mitigate two new medium severity (CVSSv3 5.8) vulnerabilities in the Snyk CLI and IDE plugins: CVE-2022-24441 and CVE-2022-22984. Although hard to exploit, these vulnerabilities can lead to arbitrary code execution on the host system. Learn about these risks and how to stay safe.

node.Title: Snyk expands in Asia-Pacific (APAC) with new datacenter in Sydney, Australia
node.Description: We are excited to announce the general availability of a new Asia-Pacific (APAC) datacenter in Sydney, Australia, helping local organizations secure their applications with Snyk.

node.Title: 10 AWS security considerations when migrating
node.Description: Learn about the different AWS security considerations you should make when migrating to Amazon Web Services.

node.Title: Can gamification unite development and security?
node.Description: Gamification is a great way to make security exciting for developers. Learn about a few ways guests from The Secure Developer have gamified security.

node.Title: How to use GitHub Actions environment variables
node.Description: This hands-on article discusses the environment variables available within GitHub Actions and when we should use them.

node.Title: How to write tests in Python using doctest
node.Description: In this article, we’ll walk through setup, write, and test your first doctest in Python — giving you all the information you need to get started.

node.Title: Writing unit tests in Java
node.Description: In this post, we’ll learn how to install and use JUnit 5 to write unit tests for some Java code. We’ll use the VSCode integrated development environment (IDE) for writing our tests and Java 11 with Maven to execute them.

node.Title: Setting up SSL/TLS for Kubernetes Ingress
node.Description: This article demonstrates how to configure TLS/SSL certificates with the Ingress controller in Kubernetes. We’ll set up an NGINX Ingress controller, create a self-signed SSL/TLS certificate, create the necessary rules to link the SSL/TLS certificate to the controller, and hook it up to a Kubernetes sample app service.

node.Title: Dependency injection in JavaScript
node.Description: In this article, you'll learn what dependency injection is, when you should use it, and what popular JavaScript frameworks it's implemented in.

node.Title: Best practices for Kubernetes Secrets management
node.Description: In this article, we’ll explore best practices for Kubernetes Secret management. Check out our Kubernetes security article for more security risks and best practices.

node.Title: How Atlassian used Snyk to solve Log4Shell
node.Description: In this post, we’ll recap Walz’s experience using Snyk to detect and remediate Log4Shell at Atlassian, as well as Silverman’s more in-depth talk about the impact of Log4Shell.

node.Title: How community participation can enhance your development career
node.Description: In this article, we’ll talk about some opportunities for community participationa few ways you can give back to your community while also developing your career.

node.Title: 5 best practices for building modern access control for cloud applications
node.Description: Learn best practices for building modern access control for cloud applications, covering RBAC, security and compliace, IAM, access control layers, reducing attack surface area, and more.

node.Title: Snyk’s ultimate AWS re:Invent 2022 survival guide
node.Description: We asked a few of the AWS re:Invent conference veterans on our team to share some words of wisdom in preparation for this year’s expo. Read on to see their tips on surviving Vegas, and making the most of your time at AWS re:Invent 2022.

node.Title: Fetch the Flag CTF 2022 writeup: Potty Training
node.Description: Learn how to solve the Potty Training challenge from Snyk's 2022 Fetch the Flag CTF competition.

node.Title: Cloud security fundamentals part 5: measure what matters
node.Description: A discussion of the fifth of five fundamentals of cloud security: measuring what matters. (Part five in a five-part series.)

node.Description: Learn how to solve the Disposable Message challenge from Snyk's 2022 Fetch the Flag CTF competition.

node.Description: Learn how to solve the git-refs challenge from Snyk's 2022 Fetch the Flag CTF competition.

node.Description: Learn how to solve the Containers are ACE challenge from Snyk's 2022 Fetch the Flag CTF competition.

node.Description: Learn how to solve the Moongoose challenge from Snyk's 2022 Fetch the Flag CTF competition.

node.Title: Implementing TLS in Java
node.Description: In this article, we’ll discuss what TLS is, what benefits it provides, and why you need it. Then we’ll walk through implementing TLS in Java.

node.Title: Announcing the open beta of Snyk’s revamped security reporting
node.Description: Today we're announcing the open beta of Snyk’s new reporting features, available to Snyk customers now. The new reporting capabilities provide improved visibility into application security risks.

node.Title: Announcing the open beta of Snyk’s revamped security reporting
node.Description: Today we're announcing the open beta of Snyk’s new reporting features, available to Snyk customers now. The new reporting capabilities provide improved visibility into application security risks.

node.Title: SnykLaunch recap: Snyk Cloud, SBOM & reporting capabilities, and customer solutions resources
node.Description: At SnykLaunch, our product leaders unveiled the latest additions to Snyk’s suite of developer-first products — including the announcement of Snyk Cloud. In this SnykLaunch recap, we'll catch you up on the new and exciting features coming to Snyk.

node.Title: NPM security: preventing supply chain attacks
node.Description: In this post, I intend to unveil npm security practices and tooling available for you as a JavaScript developer (TypeScript developers are welcome too).

node.Title: Key points from Google and Accenture’s ransomware white paper
node.Description: Author’s from Accenture and Google Cloud recently released a white paper, which details the current state of ransomware and solutions to address this growing problem. This article will provide an overview of the key points from the white paper.

node.Title: Breaking down the ‘critical’ OpenSSL vulnerability
node.Description: In this post we’ll break down the two OpenSSL vulnerabilities, look at whether or not the level of attention this received is warranted, and how concerned we should actually be.

node.Title: A day in the life of a CISO: Chris Hughes of Aquia
node.Description: Find out what a day in the life of a CISO and co-founder is like with this discussion between Chris Hughes (CIS & Co-Founder) of Aquia and Vandana Verma of Snyk.

node.Title: Secure Python URL validation
node.Description: In this article, we’ll explore the challenges of corrupted URLs, how they can damage your applications, and ways to tackle the problem.

node.Title: Cloud security fundamentals part 4: Align and automate with policy as code
node.Description: A discussion of the fourth of five fundamentals of cloud security: aligning and automating with policy as code. (Part four in a five-part series.)

node.Title: Update: OpenSSL high severity vulnerabilities
node.Description: OpenSSL has released two high severity vulnerabilities — CVE-2022-3602 and CVE-2022-3786 — related to buffer overrun.

node.Title: Ruby on Rails Docker for local development environment
node.Description: If you’ve been looking for an effective way to establish a Ruby on Rails Docker setup for your local development environment, then this post is for you. It’s a continuation of our previous article on how to install Ruby in a macOS for local development.

node.Title: Scaling your security team without hiring
node.Description: The increasing popularity of cloud technology has created a growing need for effective cloud security. Instead of simply expanding the size of security teams, organizations can improve their cloud security posture using policy as code.

node.Title: How to create SBOMs in Java with Maven and Gradle
node.Description: Learn how to easily create a software bill of materials (SBOM) for your Java applications in Maven and Gradle.

node.Title: Top 5 scary AWS misconfigurations
node.Description: Here are the top five AWS misconfigurations you should be aware of to prevent potential security gaps in your infrastructure.

node.Title: New OpenSSL critical vulnerability: What you need to know
node.Description: An upcoming release of OpenSSL, scheduled for November 1, 2022, addresses a critical security vulnerability. This post explains how to detect the vulnerability in your code and describes mitigation steps.

node.Title: Why fuzzing tools should be part of your security toolkit
node.Description: To understand how fuzzing tools improve security, let’s explore the benefits of fuzzing, discuss some use cases for fuzzing, and review an example of how fuzzing would work in a real-world test.

node.Title: How to write your first unit test in JavaScript
node.Description: Testing code is the first step to making it secure. One of the best ways to do this is to use unit tests, ensuring that each of the smaller functions within an app behave as they should — especially when the app receives edge-case or invalid inputs, or inputs that are potentially harmful.

node.Title: Understanding DNS attacks: Identifying and patching vulnerabilities
node.Description: Let's explore the most common DNS attacks and review mitigation strategies to avoid them.

node.Title: Node.js multithreading with worker threads series: worker_threads tutorial
node.Description: Multi-threading can offer substantial performance improvements for CPU-bound workflows by allowing arbitrary work to be performed in parallel. Although Node.js doesn't offer real multi-threading, you can create something similar with the worker threads module. This article will explain what it does, and show how to use it in a few real-world applications.

node.Title: Snyk’s new native integration with Bitbucket Cloud emphasizes dev-first security
node.Description: Snyk is excited to announce a new, native integration with Atlassian Bitbucket Cloud. This new release improves Snyk’s functionality within Bitbucket Cloud, making installation faster, and easier to implement.

node.Title: A Message from Snyk CEO Peter McKay
node.Description: Below is a message that was sent out to all Snyk employees today from Snyk CEO Peter McKay. Snykers, Today is one of the most difficult in Snyk’s history. Over the course of 2022 while our business has continued to grow, we’ve also witnessed a number of significant market shifts, and, as a result, we are

node.Title: How to send Snyk vulnerability data to the New Relic observability platform
node.Description: Learn to leverage the different options that the Snyk platform provides to send all your application security vulnerabilities found by Snyk directly to your New Relic observability platform

node.Title: Cloud security fundamentals part 3: Empower your developers
node.Description: A discussion of the third of five fundamentals of cloud security: empowering your developers. (Part three in a five-part series.)

node.Title: How to make a mock API server in JavaScript
node.Description: In this article, you'll learn more about mock API servers, the tools you can use to create mock APIs, how you can use them to speed up your development and testing, and how to set up a simple mock server.

node.Title: SREs bring ORDER(R) to CHAOS
node.Description: In this post, Snyk Ambassador Keith McDuffee will lead us through a discussion on how site reliability engineers (SREs) bring order to the chaos of development.

node.Title: Reviewing CVE-2022-42889: The Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability in Apache Commons Text
node.Description: In this post we'll review CVE-2022-42889, the recent Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability in Apache Commons Text, including what it is, how to remediate it, and why it's not the new Log4Shell.

node.Title: 2022 Snyk Customer Value Study highlights: The impact of developer-first security
node.Description: Learn about the 2022 Snyk Customer Value Study. The goal of this study was to understand our customers’ most important value drivers, discover how they think about their return on investment and find out how much quantifiable benefit they’ve realized by leveraging the Snyk platform.

node.Title: Secure JavaScript URL validation
node.Description: When developers need to handle URLs, we often turn to Java. However, its frequent use motivates attackers to exploit its vulnerabilities. This risk of exploitation is why we must implement URL validation in our JavaScript applications.

node.Title: How to contribute to open source projects
node.Description: As a developer, you probably rely on open source every day. But have you considered going a step further and contributing to open source projects as well? This post will cover the major reasons why you might choose to contribute to — instead of simply using —  open source projects.

node.Title: Implementing TLS/SSL in Python
node.Description: In this article, we’ll explore TLS and how to use Python to check for a website’s TLS certificate validity. Then, we’ll walk through the steps for adding TLS to your Python application on Linux.

node.Title: How to add Playwright tests to your pull request CI with GitHub Actions
node.Description: In this article you will learn the basics of how to write end-to-end tests with Playwright, running tests from GitHub Actions, deploying to Netlify, and preserving debug traces.

node.Title: Cloud security fundamentals part 2: Prevention and secure design
node.Description: A discussion of the second of five fundamentals of cloud security: prevention and secure design. (Part two in a five-part series.)

node.Title: Ruby email rule removed from Snyk Code
node.Description: You spoke up and we listened (and we double-checked in our data). Find out why we removed Snyk Code's HardcodedEmail rule for Ruby and what this means for you.

node.Title: Secure your application from Argo CD to Kubernetes
node.Description: How to use Snyk for application security in GitOps, focusing on the popular Kubernetes-native Argo CD tool.

node.Title: Improving code quality with linting in Python
node.Description: In this hands-on article, we’ll explore how fast and easy it is to perform quick linting checks in Python using Pylint — one of the most popular linting tools. We’ll also see how linting code can help us adhere to the PEP8 code style guide.

node.Title: How to use Java DTOs to stay secure
node.Description: In this article, I will explain how DTOs are used in modern Java applications, ways your application can benefit, and how Java DTOs can help you be more secure by preventing accidental data leaks.

node.Title: Security lessons with a Snyk Ambassador
node.Description: A post from Ambassador Andres Haro, describing lessons learned from a recent security incident and how you can protect your application from similar attacks.

node.Title: SnykWeek New York: In the cloud
node.Description: At SnykWeek New York, we heard lots of conversation about cloud security. The day also included a peek at our product roadmap and a fun interactive hacking session. We love #snykweek.

node.Title: Container images simplified with Google Ko
node.Description: In this article, we’ll look at using Google Ko to build container images without Dockerfiles, SBOMs, and integrating with Kubernetes.

node.Title: Red teams vs blue teams: Breaking down security roles
node.Description: Red teams vs blue teams — we've heard the terms, but what does it all mean? This post will cover the basics of red, blue, and purple teams, and explain how they work together to enhance an organization’s security posture.

node.Title: Cloud security fundamentals part 1: Know your environment
node.Description: A discussion of the first of five fundamentals of cloud security: knowing your environment. (Part one in a five-part series.)

node.Title: Proxy authentication in Snyk CLI for Windows
node.Description: We’re announcing support for proxy authentication in Snyk CLI for Windows.

node.Title: Snyk + International Dog Day: recap
node.Description: A recap of Snyk's 2022 International Dog Day campaign, including a look at some of the custom dog sketches for our sweepstakes winners.

node.Title: 2022 Collaboration Partner of the Year: Snyk
node.Description: This week, at HashiConf 2022, Snyk was recognized as a HashiCorp Partner of the Year for Collaboration Technology.

node.Title: Phony PyPi package imitates known developer
node.Description: A recent interesting finding in the Python Package Index (PyPi) attempted to imitate a known open source developer through identity spoofing. Upon further analysis, the team uncovered that the package, raw-tool, was attempting to hide malicious behavior using base64 encoding, reaching out to malicious servers, and executing obfuscated code. In this post, we’re going to take a deeper look at that vulnerability, but first let’s take a look at how our researchers discovered it.

node.Title: Snyk named a 2022 Gartner Peer Insights Customers’ Choice for Application Security Testing
node.Description: We're excited to announce that Snyk was named a Customers’ Choice in the 2022 Gartner Peer Insights ‘Voice of the Customer’: Application Security Testing. This distinction is based on meeting or exceeding overall rating, user interest, and adoption.

node.Title: Command injection vulnerability in Snyk CLI released prior to September 1, 2022 (older than v1.996.0)
node.Description: As a Snyk user, we want to let you know about a medium severity vulnerability (CVSSv3 6.4) in our CLI that you should be aware of: CVE-2022-40764.

node.Title: Why developers hold the key to cloud security
node.Description: A look at the advantages of empowering developers with the right tools and processes for mastering cloud security.

node.Title: Choosing the best Node.js Docker image
node.Description: Snyk's Liran Tal discusses strategies for choosing the best node.js Docker image.

node.Title: Supply chain security and Executive Order M-21-30
node.Description: On September 14, the White House released Executive Order M-21-30, emphasizing and reminding us that there are NIST guidelines for securing any software being sold to the US Government. In this post, we'll recap the EO, it's impact, and how Snyk can help your organization comply.

node.Title: Introducing the new Snyk UI
node.Description: We're excited to announce that a new Snyk UI is coming soon! Starting October 12th, 2022 we’ll be rolling out some exciting new user interface changes for the Snyk application. Read on for a sneak peek.

node.Title: Snyk & 01Founders partner to diversify tech
node.Description: On September 15, Snyk kick off our partnership with 01Founders by hosting 30 learners at our London office. The event featured a panel discussion, capture the flag challenge, and networking opportunities with dozens of Snykers. Read on for a recap of the day!

How to setup a local development environment that doesn’t conflict with brew or an existing macOS Ruby version.

node.Title: Response to the Enduring Security Framework (ESF) Guide for Developers

This is exactly why the recent publication, Recommended Practices Guide for Developers by the The National Security Agency (NSA), Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), and the Office of the Director of

node.Title: The npm faker package and the unexpected demise of open source libraries
node.Description: Liran Tal discusses real-world incidents that demonstrate how even the mightiest of open source projects can be defeated. He also addresses the continuous struggles of open source software sustainability, maintainer burnout, and these things affect the greater developer community.

node.Title: Solve Hack the Box and other CTF challenges with Snyk
node.Description: Hack The Box (HTB) is a platform that gamifies cybersecurity training. In this article, we'll discuss how Snyk can help you solve Hack the Box and other CTF challenges.

node.Title: Best practices for containerizing .net applications
node.Description: In this post, we'll cove some best practices for containerizing .NET applications — including those on the 4.x version framework. We’ll also discuss using small images and image scanning, to reduce security risks and remove unnecessary components from our containers.

node.Title: How to build a Slack bot with Zapier and JavaScript to fetch trending topics on Reddit
node.Description: In this article you'll learn, how to build a Slack bot integration, create Triggers and Actions workflows in Zapier to post messages to a Slack channel, use a Reddit API key to access posts using native Reddit Access Tokens and Refresh Tokens, and more.

node.Title: Securing container applications using the Snyk CLI
node.Description: We are excited to share that now, when using the snyk container test/monitor commands, we will scan for application vulnerabilities by default.

node.Title: Best practices for container isolation
node.Description: This article explores the implications of container isolation and outlines best practices for security and methodology for Linux, sandbox, and virtualized containers.

node.Title: Applying the principle of least privilege to Kubernetes using RBAC
node.Description: In this article, we’ll explore how Kubernetes (K8s) supports PoLP by implementing role-based access control.

node.Title: Best practices for managing Java dependencies
node.Description: In this article, I’ll give you some advice and best practices for dealing with Java dependencies in your project.

node.Title: Building a secure API with gRPC
node.Description: This article will demonstrate how to use gRPC via a client and server-like communication between two Node.js applications. We’ll also highlight some safety measures when using gRPC as the communication mechanism in your services.

node.Title: Keeping Docker secrets secure (even if you’re not using Kubernetes)
node.Description: This article discusses managing secrets in Docker, exploring best practices and potential risks with their use.

node.Title: 8 tips for securing containers from source to runtime
node.Description: Today we’re announcing a new container security cheat sheet and report — created in collaboration with our partner Sysdig. In this post, we’ll outline tips to help you successfully navigate the challenges of container security with a focus on three core principles.

node.Title: Rediscovering argument injection when using VCS tools — git and mercurial
node.Description: In this blog post, we aim to demonstrate common scenarios where, even when using a safe API, it's possible to execute arbitrary commands through injection argument options.

node.Title: 5 ways to secure Drupal 10
node.Description: While the transition from Drupal 9 to Drupal l 10 is expected to be smooth, we can’t ignore security. So, in this article, we'll explore 5 ways to secure Drupal 10.

node.Title: How open source C++ code can introduce security risks
node.Description: This article explores how open source code introduces vulnerabilities into the software supply chain. It also highlights how you can identify vulnerabilities in C++ open source security using automated vulnerability scanners.

node.Title: Ruby gem installations can expose you to lockfile injection attacks
node.Description: In this post, we’ll look at the security blindspots of lockfile injection that a Ruby gem might expose via its Gemfile.lock.

node.Title: Top 5 C++ security risks
node.Description: This article explores the top five C++ security concerns that affect code development and offers advice on mitigating them.

node.Title: Snyk finds PyPi malware that steals Discord and Roblox credential and payment info
node.Description: Learn about the newly discovered PyPi malware that attempts to steal credential and payment information from Discord and Roblox users.

node.Title: Installing multiple Snyk Kubernetes controllers into a single Kubernetes cluster
node.Description: Learn how to secure container images and workloads across Kubernetes clusters with Snyk.

node.Title: Introducing Snyk Training, our online learning platform
node.Description: Snyk has officially launched Snyk Training, a free online resource to help developers and security teams learn how to implement, configure, and use Snyk on their own.

node.Title: Controlling your server with a reverse shell attack
node.Description: Snyk's Senior Developer Advocate, Brian Vermeer, discusses how reverse shell attacks work and how you can protect yourself from them.

node.Title: Introducing Snyk Auto-Issues for Jira: a new open source application for automated issue creation
node.Description: We're excite to introduce our new Jira app: Snyk Auto-Issues for Jira. This open source application automatically creates new Jira issues for security vulnerabilities that Snyk discovers, helping to streamline vulnerability management.

node.Title: Snyk ranked #20 on 2022 Forbes Cloud 100 list
node.Description: On behalf of the entire Snyk community, I am excited to share that Forbes has named Snyk to the Forbes Cloud 100 list for the third consecutive year, coming in at #20  — which is 19 spots higher than last year! The full list was unveiled this morning. The evolving need for secure digital transformation

node.Title: AWS re:Inforce 2022 recap
node.Description: If you're looking to catch up on what happened at this years AWS re:Inforce, this is the blog for you. There were many important announcements were this year, including some exciting updates on the cloud security front. In this post, we'll quickly review the goals of the conference and who should attend, before diving into the keynote highlights, software updates, and helpful resources.

node.Title: A definitive guide to Ruby gems dependency management
node.Description: Ruby has an entire ecosystem of third-party open source libraries which it refers to as gems, or sometimes Ruby gems. In this article, I'll run through the concepts and tooling that make up the Ruby dependencies ecosystem, and answer some of the common questions Ruby developers have.

node.Title: Securing PHP containers
node.Description: Too often, developers assume that container isolation means containers are inherently secure. This incorrect assumption leads to complacency. In this post, we'll explore some best practices for securing PHP containers and avoiding common pitfalls.

node.Title: Slidev 101: Coding presentations with Markdown
node.Description: Building presentations can be a tedious process. Thankfully, there’s a better and more programmatic way of building your slides — Slidev, the open source project that allows you to code your presentations.

node.Title: Testing effectively in Terraform
node.Description: Terraform is an infrastructure as code (IaC) solution that enables DevOps teams to deploy on-premise or public cloud infrastructure components. Today, we'll discuss some common strategies for testing effectively in Terraform.

node.Title: Join the Snyk Developer Challenge
node.Description: We’re excited to announce the Snyk Developer Challenge! This event gives conference attendees a chance to flex their security skills by fixing vulnerabilities to earn points on the leaderboard. At the end of the conference, the two highest scoring participants will be crowned the winners and receive a special prize.

node.Title: Welcoming Manoj Nair: Snyk’s New Chief Product Officer
node.Description: We're thrilled to welcome Manoj Nair joins Snyk today as our new Chief Product Officer. In this pivotal phase of Snyk’s growth, Manoj will lead our global product team, setting our comprehensive product roadmap and strategy in both the short and long-term.

node.Title: Buffer overflow attacks in C++: A hands-on guide
node.Description: A buffer overflow attack is the exploitation of a buffer overflow vulnerability, typically by a malicious actor who wants to gain access or information. In this post, we'll explain how a buffer overflow occurs and show you how to protect your C++ code from these attacks.

node.Title: Allyship and advocacy: Celebrating Disability Pride Month at Snyk
node.Description: o celebrate Disability Pride Month, Snyk's Director of Inclusion, Equity, and Diversity, Ashley Ladd (she/her), sat down with Alex Fallon (they/them) to discuss what Disability Pride means to them, how able-bodied people can be better allies and advocates for the Disabled community, inspirational figures, helpful resources, and so much more.

node.Title: The Cloud Security Podcast is now powered by Snyk!
node.Description: We are excited to announce that the Cloud Security Podcast is now powered by Snyk!

node.Title: Best practices for your first 30 days with Snyk
node.Description: Adopting a new platform can seem intimidating, but with Snyk it doesn't have to be. We have three tips to help you roll out Snyk, and have a seamless and successful first 30 days across your business or enterprise.

node.Title: Webinar recap: Snyk and the new era of software security
node.Description: Snyk’s Senior Product Marketing Manager, Frank Fischer, recently hosted a webinar about the value in using a developer security platform to secure code, dependencies, containers, and infrastructure as code (IaC). In this blog post, we’ll highlight some of the key insights from the presentation.

node.Title: Addressing cybersecurity challenges in open source software with the Linux Foundation
node.Description: Snyk recently partnered with the Linux Foundation to produce a report focusing on the state of security in the OSS space. Following the report's publication, experts from Snyk held a webinar with the Linux Foundation to discuss some of the key insights, read on for a recap.

node.Title: Top 5 tips for C++ security
node.Description: C++ has become a pivotal part of the modern day tech industry. As with any widely adopted or user based development, it’s important to ensure that elements of security have been integrated throughout the application. So, let's look at some security tips to keep in mind when building with C++.

node.Title: Webinar recap: The missing story with every cloud breach
node.Description: Snyk’s Chief Architect, Josh Stella, recently hosted a webinar about cloud security. During this talk, he discussed the missing story in every cloud breach: the tale of how, when, and where attackers operate in the cloud. He also revealed a methodology for securing cloud resources against modern cloud attacks.

node.Title: 10 React security best practices
node.Description: Looking for the best ways to secure your React app? Then you’ve come to the right place! We’ve created this checklist of React security best practices to help you and your team find and fix security issues in your React applications. We’ll also show you how to automatically test your React code for security-related issues

node.Title: Improving developer experience with security tools at Pinterest
node.Description: Simon Maple from Snyk and Kalpesh Dharwadkar from Pinterest discuss using Snyk's developer-friendly tools to integrate good security practices into the software development workflow.

node.Title: The security course missing from higher education
node.Description: We ask why high school and college computer science programs are lacking content on cybersecurity.
